sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Current state:
- Left box: c at [28-31, 16-19] (reverted to inactive)
- Player: N at [28-30, 20-23]
- White box: W at [31-35, 20-23] (activated)
- Box right: c at [32-35, 36-39] (still inactive)
- HUD: c at [63-63, 42-63]
So the box below was activated (W), and the left box reverted to c. Now I need to activate the box right at [32-35, 36-39].
The player is at [28-30, 20-23] and the white box is at [31-35, 20-23]. The box right is at [32-35, 36-39].
To activate the box right:
1. SPACE to deactivate the current white box (W→G)
2. Move RIGHT to select the box right (c→G)
3. SPACE to activate it (G→W)
But wait, after SPACE, the white box becomes G at [31-35, 20-23]. Then moving RIGHT would put the player at [28-30, 24-27] (or similar), and the G box would follow to [31-35, 24-27]. The box right is at [32-35, 36-39]. The player would need to move RIGHT multiple times to get to the box right.
Let me try: SPACE, RIGHT, RIGHT, RIGHT, SPACE.
Actually, let me think about this more carefully. The player is at [28-30, 20-23] (3x4). Moving RIGHT puts the player at [28-30, 24-27]. Moving RIGHT again puts the player at [28-30, 28-31]. Moving RIGHT again puts the player at [28-30, 32-35]. Moving RIGHT again puts the player at [28-30, 36-39], which overlaps with the box right at [32-35, 36-39] (no row overlap, since player is at rows 28-30 and box is at rows 32-35). So the player can move RIGHT multiple times.
But the white box (or G box) follows the player. After SPACE, the white box becomes G at [31-35, 20-23]. When the player moves RIGHT, the G box follows to [31-35, 24-27], then [31-35, 28-31], etc.
The box right is at [32-35, 36-39]. The G box at [31-35, 32-35] would overlap with the box right at [32-35, 36-39] (rows 32-35 overlap, cols 32-35 vs 36-39 don't overlap). So no overlap.
But when the player moves RIGHT to [28-30, 36-39], the G box would be at [31-35, 36-39], which overlaps with the box right at [32-35, 36-39] (rows 32-35 overlap, cols 36-39 overlap). So the G box would block the player from moving RIGHT.
Hmm, but G boxes don't seem to block movement (that's why the SPACE trick works). Let me just try it.
Actually, wait. The SPACE trick works because when you press SPACE, the W box becomes G, and then you can move through it. But the G box still follows the player. So when the player moves RIGHT, the G box follows, and if the G box overlaps with another box, that might block movement.
Let me try a different approach. What if I move RIGHT first (without SPACE), then SPACE, then continue RIGHT?
1. RIGHT: player at [28-30, 24-27], white box at [31-35, 24-27]
2. RIGHT: player at [28-30, 28-31], white box at [31-35, 28-31]
3. RIGHT: player at [28-30, 32-35], white box at [31-35, 32-35]
4. RIGHT: player at [28-30, 36-39], white box at [31-35, 36-39] - this overlaps with box right at [32-35, 36-39], so blocked
So I can only move RIGHT 3 times before the white box blocks.
What if I: RIGHT, RIGHT, RIGHT, SPACE, RIGHT?
1. RIGHT: player at [28-30, 24-27], white box at [31-35, 24-27]
2. RIGHT: player at [28-30, 28-31], white box at [31-35, 28-31]
3. RIGHT: player at [28-30, 32-35], white box at [31-35, 32-35]
4. SPACE: white box becomes G at [31-35, 32-35]
5. RIGHT: player at [28-30, 36-39], G box at [31-35, 36-39] - overlaps with box right at [32-35, 36-39]
The G box at [31-35, 36-39] overlaps with the box right at [32-35, 36-39] (rows 32-35, cols 36-39). But G boxes don't block, so maybe this works?
Actually, I think the issue is that the G box and the c box overlap. The game might not allow two boxes to occupy the same space, regardless of color.
Let me try a different approach. What if I move the player ABOVE the box right, then move LEFT to select it?
1. UP: player at [25-27, 20-23], white box at [28-32, 20-23] (or similar)
2. RIGHT, RIGHT, RIGHT: player at [25-27, 32-35], white box at [28-32, 32-35]
3. RIGHT: player at [25-27, 36-39], white box at [28-32, 36-39] - white box overlaps with box right at [32-35, 36-39] (row 32 overlaps)
Hmm, still blocked.
What if I move the player way above, then RIGHT, then DOWN to select the box right from above?
1. UP, UP, UP: player at [22-24, 20-23], white box at [25-29, 20-23]
2. RIGHT, RIGHT, RIGHT, RIGHT: player at [22-24, 36-39], white box at [25-29, 36-39] - white box doesn't overlap with box right at [32-35, 36-39] (rows 25-29 vs 32-35, no overlap)
3. DOWN: player at [25-27, 36-39], white box at [28-32, 36-39] - white box overlaps with box right at [32-35, 36-39] (row 32 overlaps)
Still blocked.
What if I: UP, UP, UP, RIGHT, RIGHT, RIGHT, RIGHT, DOWN, DOWN, DOWN?
1. UP, UP, UP: player at [19-21, 20-23], white box at [22-26, 20-23]
2. RIGHT, RIGHT, RIGHT, RIGHT: player at [19-21, 36-39], white box at [22-26, 36-39]
3. DOWN: player at [22-24, 36-39], white box at [25-29, 36-39] - no overlap with box right
4. DOWN: player at [25-27, 36-39], white box at [28-32, 36-39] - white box overlaps with box right (row 32)
Still blocked.
I think the issue is that the white box always follows the player, and when the player gets close to the box right, the white box overlaps with it.
Let me try the SPACE trick: SPACE first to deactivate the white box, then move RIGHT multiple times, then DOWN to select the box right.
1. SPACE: white box becomes G at [31-35, 20-23]
2. RIGHT, RIGHT, RIGHT, RIGHT: player at [28-30, 36-39], G box at [31-35, 36-39] - G box overlaps with box right
But G boxes don't block, so maybe this works? Let me try.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# SPACE to deactivate white box, then RIGHT to approach box right
result = action(['SPACE', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT'])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("After SPACE, RIGHTx3:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['c', 'G', 'W', 'N'] and n['pixels'] >= 8:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  color={n['color']}, rows [{min(rows)}-{max(rows)}], cols [{min(cols)}-{max(cols)}], px={n['pixels']}")

# Try RIGHT again
result2 = action(['RIGHT'])
seg2 = current_frame.segmentation
print("\nAfter RIGHT:")
for n in seg2['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['c', 'G', 'W', 'N'] and n['pixels'] >= 8:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  color={n['color']}, rows [{min(rows)}-{max(rows)}], cols [{min(cols)}-{max(cols)}], px={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
